# services.silverbullet

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.silverbullet`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.silverbullet.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Silverbullet, an open-source, self-hosted, offline-capable Personal Knowledge Management (PKM) web application. |
| `services.silverbullet.envFile` | `null or absolute path` | File containing extra environment variables. For example: `SB_USER=user:password SB_AUTH_TOKEN=abcdefg12345` |
| `services.silverbullet.extraArgs` | `list of string` | Extra arguments passed to silverbullet. |
| `services.silverbullet.group` | `string` | The group to run Silverbullet under. By default, a group named `silverbullet` will be created. |
| `services.silverbullet.listenAddress` | `string` | Address or hostname to listen on. Defaults to 127.0.0.1. |
| `services.silverbullet.listenPort` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Port to listen on. |
| `services.silverbullet.openFirewall` | `boolean` | Open port in the firewall. |
| `services.silverbullet.package` | `package` | The silverbullet package to use. |
| `services.silverbullet.spaceDir` | `absolute path` | Folder to store Silverbullet's space/workspace. By default it is located at `/var/lib/silverbullet`. |
| `services.silverbullet.user` | `string` | The user to run Silverbullet as. By default, a user named `silverbullet` will be created whose space directory is [spaceDir](#opt-services.silverbullet.spaceDir). |
